input.question:
We are a student engineering team designing a vehicle for the FSAE competition. Attached is the FSAE rules document. Please list all rules relevant to `Aerodynamic/Aerodynamics`. Answer with only the rule numbers (i.e.: AA.1.1.1) separated by commas and no other words.



The rules relevant to `Aerodynamic/Aerodynamics` are:

retrieved_context:
RULE: in.8.2.3
            TITLE: if any vehicle on track is observed to have large, uncontrolled movements of aerodynamic

            CONTENT:
            rule IN.8.2.3: If any vehicle on track is observed to have large, uncontrolled movements of Aerodynamic
IN.8.2.3 If any vehicle on track is observed to have large, uncontrolled movements of Aerodynamic 
Devices, then officials may Black Flag the vehicle for IN.15 Reinspection.

RULE: t.7.7.1
            TITLE: any part of any aerodynamic device that is located:

            CONTENT:
            rule T.7.7.1: Any part of any Aerodynamic Device that is located:
T.7.7.1 Any part of any Aerodynamic Device that is located:   
a. In the Rear Aerodynamic Zone must be no higher than 1200 mm above the ground  
b. Outside of the Rear Aerodynamic Zone must be no higher than 500 mm above the 
ground
Formula SAE® Rules 2024 © 2023 SAE International  Page 71 of 140   
Version 1.0   1 Sep 2023 
c. Forward of the centerline of the front wheel axles and outboard of two vertical planes 
parallel to the centerline of the chassis touching the inside of the front tires at the height 
of the hubs must be no higher than 250 mm above the ground

RULE: t.7.1.3
            TITLE: all aerodynamic devices must meet:

            CONTENT:
            rule T.7.1.3: All Aerodynamic Devices must meet:
T.7.1.3 All Aerodynamic Devices must meet:  
a. The mounting system provides sufficient rigidity in the static condition  
b. The Aerodynamic Devices do not oscillate or move excessively when the vehicle is 
moving.   Refer to IN.8.2

RULE: t.7.1.1
            TITLE: aerodynamic device

            CONTENT:
            rule T.7.1.1: Aerodynamic Device
T.7.1.1 Aerodynamic Device 
A part on the vehicle which guides airflow for purposes including generation of downforce 
and/or change of drag.   
Examples include but are not limited to: wings, undertray, splitter, endplates, vanes

RULE: t.7.3.3
            TITLE: rear aerodynamic zone

            CONTENT:
            rule T.7.3.3: Rear Aerodynamic Zone
T.7.3.3 Rear Aerodynamic Zone 
The volume that is: 
• Rearward of the Head Restraint Plane 
• Inboard of two vertical planes parallel to the centerline of the chassis touching the inside 
of the rear tires at the height of the hub centerline

RULE: t.7.2.2
            TITLE: bodywork, a nose cone, or another component mounted to the vehicle is an aerodynamic

            CONTENT:
            rule T.7.2.2: Bodywork, a nose cone, or another component mounted to the vehicle is an Aerodynamic
T.7.2.2 Bodywork, a nose cone, or another component mounted to the vehicle is an Aerodynamic 
Device if is designed to, or may possibly, produce force due to aerodynamic effects

RULE: t.7.4
            TITLE: location

            CONTENT:
            rule T.7.4: Location
T.7.4 Location  
Any part of any Aerodynamic Device or Bodywork must meet V.1.1 and V.1.4.1

RULE: t.7.3.1
            TITLE: all aerodynamic device limitations are measured:

            CONTENT:
            rule T.7.3.1: All Aerodynamic Device limitations are measured:
T.7.3.1 All Aerodynamic Device limitations are measured: 
a. With the wheels pointing in the straight ahead position 
b. Without a driver in the vehicle 
The intent is to standardize the measurement, see GR.6.4.1

RULE: in.8.2.1
            TITLE: any aerodynamic devices may be checked by pushing on the device in any direction and at

            CONTENT:
            rule IN.8.2.1: Any Aerodynamic Devices may be checked by pushing on the device in any direction and at
IN.8.2.1 Any Aerodynamic Devices may be checked by pushing on the device in any direction and at 
any point.   
The following is guidance, but actual conformance will be up to technical inspectors at the 
respective competitions. The intent is to reduce the likelihood of wings detaching

RULE: t.7.2.1
            TITLE: conventionally designed bodywork or a nose cone is not considered an aerodynamic device

            CONTENT:
            rule T.7.2.1: Conventionally designed Bodywork or a nose cone is not considered an Aerodynamic Device
T.7.2.1 Conventionally designed Bodywork or a nose cone is not considered an Aerodynamic Device

RULE: t.7.6
            TITLE: width

            CONTENT:
            rule T.7.6: Width
T.7.6 Width 
In plan view, any part of any Aerodynamic Device must be:

RULE: t.7.6.3
            TITLE: when rearward of the centerline of the rear wheel axles:

            CONTENT:
            rule T.7.6.3: When rearward of the centerline of the rear wheel axles:
T.7.6.3 When rearward of the centerline of the rear wheel axles: 
In the Rear Aerodynamic Zone

RULE: t.7.5
            TITLE: length

            CONTENT:
            rule T.7.5: Length
T.7.5 Length 
In plan view, any part of any Aerodynamic Device must be:  
a. No more than 700 mm forward of the fronts of the front tires 
b. No more than 250 mm rearward of the rear of the rear tires

RULE: t.1.3.3
            TITLE: all moving suspension and steering components and other sharp edges inside the cockpit

            CONTENT:
            rule T.1.3.3: All moving suspension and steering components and other sharp edges inside the cockpit
T.1.3.3 All moving suspension and steering components and other sharp edges inside the cockpit 
between the Front Hoop and a vertical plane 100 mm rearward of the pedals must be covered 
by a shield made of a solid material.  
Moving components include, but are not limited to springs, shock absorbers, rocker arms, anti-
roll/sway bars, steering racks and steering column CV joints.

RULE: t.1.3.2
            TITLE: while the driver’s feet are touching the pedals, in side and front views, any part of the driver’s

            CONTENT:
            rule T.1.3.2: While the driver’s feet are touching the pedals, in side and front views, any part of the driver’s
T.1.3.2 While the driver’s feet are touching the pedals, in side and front views, any part of the driver’s 
feet or legs must not extend above or outside of the Major Structure of the Chassis.

RULE: t.1.6.2
            TITLE: insulation may be external to the cockpit or incorporated with the driver’s seat or firewall.

            CONTENT:
            rule T.1.6.2: Insulation may be external to the cockpit or incorporated with the Driver’s Seat or Firewall.
T.1.6.2 Insulation may be external to the cockpit or incorporated with the Driver’s Seat or Firewall.
Formula SAE® Rules 2024 © 2023 SAE International  Page 57 of 140   
Version 1.0   1 Sep 2023

RULE: t.1.2.2
            TITLE: conduct of the test.  the template:

            CONTENT:
            rule T.1.2.2: Conduct of the test.  The template:
T.1.2.2 Conduct of the test.  The template:  
a. Will be held vertically and inserted into the cockpit opening rearward of the rearmost 
portion of the steering column.   
b. Will then be passed horizontally through the cockpit to a point 100 mm rearwards of the 
face of the rearmost pedal when in the inoperative position 
c. May be moved vertically inside the cockpit

RULE: gr.1.4.4
            TITLE: each design will be judged and evaluated against other competing designs in a series of static

            CONTENT:
            rule GR.1.4.4: Each design will be judged and evaluated against other competing designs in a series of Static
GR.1.4.4 Each design will be judged and evaluated against other competing designs in a series of Static 
and Dynamic events to determine the vehicle that best meets the design goals and may be 
profitably built and marketed.

RULE: t.1.7.1
            TITLE: all vehicles must have a floor closeout to prevent track debris from entering

            CONTENT:
            rule T.1.7.1: All vehicles must have a Floor Closeout to prevent track debris from entering
T.1.7.1 All vehicles must have a Floor Closeout to prevent track debris from entering

RULE: t.1.1.1
            TITLE: the template shown below must pass through the cockpit opening

            CONTENT:
            rule T.1.1.1: The template shown below must pass through the cockpit opening
T.1.1.1 The template shown below must pass through the cockpit opening
